data.title: GrafTech Announces Third Quarter 2025 Earnings Conference Call and Webcast
data.description:
GrafTech Announces Third Quarter 2025 Earnings Conference Call and Webcast

GrafTech International Ltd. (NYSE:EAF) (the “Company”) will hold its Third Quarter 2025 Earnings Conference Call and Webcast on Friday, October 24, 2025 at 10:00 a.m. (EDT). The call will be hosted by senior management to discuss financial results for the third quarter ended September 30, 2025 and current business initiatives. These financial results will be released on October 24, 2025 before market open and will be available on our investor relations website at: http://ir.graftech.com.

The conference call dial-in number is +1 (800) 715-9871 toll-free in the United States or +1 (646) 307-1963 for international calls, conference ID: 9239453. Live audio of the conference call will be available via webcast on our website or can be accessed at https://events.q4inc.com/attendee/973707917. Archived replays of the conference call and webcast will be made available on our investor relations website at: http://ir.graftech.com.

About GrafTech

GrafTech International Ltd. is a leading manufacturer of high-quality graphite electrode products essential to the production of electric arc furnace steel and other ferrous and non-ferrous metals. The Company has a competitive portfolio of low-cost, ultra-high power graphite electrode manufacturing facilities, with some of the highest capacity facilities in the world. We are the only large-scale graphite electrode producer that is substantially vertically integrated into petroleum needle coke, our key raw material for graphite electrode manufacturing. This unique position provides us with competitive advantages in product quality and cost.

Michael Dillon
216-676-2000

data.title: This S&P 500 sector gauge points to 'excessive' confidence and imminent pullback
data.description:
By Christine Idzelis

'We believe our correlation work does a good job of signaling near term highs,' DataTrek says

The U.S. stock market closed higher Thursday, with the S&P 500 booking yet another record peak.

Sectors in the U.S. stock market just traded in a way that suggests investors are excessively confident and a pullback may be near - although that doesn't necessarily spell the end of the S&P 500's bull run, according to DataTrek Research.

Correlations for five major S&P 500 sectors hit an unusually low level that since 2023 has been followed by a drop for the U.S. equities benchmark. "Every other time they have done so since 2023," the S&P 500 has declined by 5% to 18% in the following weeks, DataTrek co-founder Nicholas Colas said in a note emailed Thursday.

He tracked the trailing 30-day price correlations for the technology, industrial, consumer discretionary, financial and healthcare sectors versus the S&P 500 in the chart below, finding they recently fell more than 2 standard deviations below the long-term average.

DATATREK RESEARCH

Since the current bull market began around late 2022 and early 2023, "abnormally low sector correlation readings" of below 0.61 line up with near-term tops for the S&P 500, Colas found.

The S&P 500 SPX ended Thursday at a fresh all-time high that marked its 30th record close of the year, with the bull market continuing amid some concerns over stretched valuations.

"S&P sector correlations currently reflect excessive investor confidence, but history shows we still need a catalyst to come along and reset them back to more rational levels," wrote Colas. Until one appears, he said that U.S. large-cap stocks may keep "slowly drifting their way higher."

Read: ETFs that protect against 'painful' stock-market drops are attracting worried investors

The U.S. stock market so far appears to be taking the U.S. government shutdown that began on Wednesday in stride. The shutdown has left investors navigating markets without fresh economic data that the government had been scheduled to report, including data on initial jobless claims that normally would have been released Thursday morning.

Meanwhile, S&P 500 sector correlations, as measured by Colas, fell last week to the unusually low levels of 0.596 on Sept. 24 and 0.607 on Sept. 25. He found that's just below the 2-standard-deviation level of 0.61, which has "served as a reliable sign of an imminent pullback since the start of 2023."

Still, DataTrek remains bullish on U.S. large-cap equities, according to his note.

"This is NOT a 'sell everything today' signal," Colas wrote. "While we believe our correlation work does a good job of signaling near term highs, we know reliably calling tops is ferociously difficult."

Check out: Betting markets like Kalshi see the government shutdown lasting 10 days or more. Investors might not like that.

DataTrek will be watching the Cboe Volatility Index to potentially signal a "sensible entry point" to buy the dip should one emerge following the low sector correlation levels that the firm identified, according to Colas.

The volatility index, which is known by the ticker symbol VIX VIX, has been trading below its long-term average of 19.5, which it tends to do during bull markets, he noted. It rose to around 16.6 on Thursday, according to FactSet data.

To Colas's thinking, "a VIX above 19.5 should signal temporary market unease," opening up a tradeable or investable event.

"Since the start of 2024, the VIX has crossed from below to above the 19.5 average on 11 occasions," he wrote. "The average 1-month gain after such a move in the VIX has been +2.2 percent, with a win rate of 82 [percent] and a maximum drawdown of -2.9 percent."

The U.S. stock market closed higher Thursday, with the S&P 500 rising 0.1%, the Dow Jones Industrial Average DJIA gaining 0.2% and the technology-heavy Nasdaq Composite COMP advancing 0.4%. All three indexes finished the trading session at fresh record peaks, according to Dow Jones Market Data.

-Christine Idzelis

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal. 




  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  10-02-25 1633ET


Copyright (c) 2025 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: Tucson Dispensary Champions Water Education to Tackle Arizona's Cannabis Resource Puzzle
data.description:
Tucson Dispensary Champions Water Education to Tackle Arizona's Cannabis Resource Puzzle

In 2025, Arizona lawmakers introduced legislation to control water use in cannabis cultivation—a crop that cannot be sold outside Arizona. D2 Dispensaries is stepping up with educational tools and transparency to support informed discussions on sustainability.

Arizona's persistent drought underscores the urgency of understanding how industries, including cannabis, impact our finite water resources. Without statewide tracking, estimates of annual cannabis water use vary widely, from hundreds of millions to potentially over a billion gallons, highlighting the need for Arizona to explore best practices from other states.

D2 has launched ecopride.com as an educational platform for Tucsonans and all Arizonans to dive into water inventory challenges, risks of inaction, cannabis water tools, and actionable steps for involvement—fostering a informed path forward.

D2 Dispensaries is leading by example in responsible practices, using precisely the water needed to supply its Tucson community. Leveraging efficient indoor setups and innovative irrigation, D2 has cut its water usage by more than 40% in the last two years. Even with some of the state's largest dispensaries, D2 keeps its operational scale compact relative to the broader industry, representing only a minimal share of overall consumption.

To promote education and accountability, D2 is openly disclosing that its production facility averaged 143,180 gallons total monthly last year for about 18,000 square feet of cultivation, and 32,000 square feet dedicated to extraction, manufacturing and kitchen needs. D2 pays monthly utility bills ranging from $2,000 - $2,200 for water, sewer, and environmental services at its production facility. D2 believes that Tucsonans will gravitate towards dispensaries who are transparent and share their water usage numbers and costs.

“Arizona’s water future hinges on informed stewardship, not guesswork,” said Moe Asnani, Co-Founder of D2 Dispensaries. “As someone who has lived in Tucson since 1999, it is tough to see how hard inflation has hit our community. We pay for our water and work hard to provide locally grown premium cannabis that won’t impact future water costs and quality of life for Tucsonans.”

Several states with legal cannabis set grow limits to align production with available resources. In Arizona, the absence of such caps has prompted calls for deeper analysis of expansion's effects on water supplies. D2 advocates for public education on these topics, especially amid statewide conservation efforts.

Recent trends show Arizona's cannabis output exceeding local demand, prompting broader conversations about balancing growth with environmental realities in this evolving sector.

“Water is life in the desert,” said Consuelo Hernandez, State Representative for Legislative District 21 who sponsored HB 2941 to control statewide cannabis water use. “Our water needs are growing everyday with agriculture, mining, chip manufacturing, AI data centers, and new residential and commercial development. I am not sure we can sustain overproducing a controlled substance that cannot even be sold outside Arizona legally.”

In parallel with water conservation, D2 pursues green advancements such as sustainable packaging and efficient lighting, delivering quality cannabis that prioritizes community and environmental health.

About D2 Dispensaries

D2 Dispensaries is a locally owned and operated cannabis company with locations in Downtown Tucson, Eastside Tucson, and Oracle & Roger. Known for quality products, large stores, and expert service, D2 is also a sustainability leader in the industry. By limiting its environmental footprint and using resources responsibly, D2 offers Tucson residents a truly eco-friendly local choice.

Media Contact
Katie Hawbaker
katie@ilava.com
646.842.1535

View source version on businesswire.com: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20251002599005/en/

data.title: China has not bought a bushel of soybeans from U.S. farmers this year. What happens to the crop now?
data.description:
By Myra P. Saefong

Trump says he plans to talk to China's president - and soybeans will be a 'major topic' of discussion

China hasn't purchased any U.S. soybeans for the 2025-26 marketing year.

For U.S. farmers, abundant rains to fuel a bumper crop would normally be good news - but it won't matter how much they produce this year if China, the world's biggest buyer of soybeans, doesn't purchase any of it.

And amid the trade wars launched by the Trump administration, the U.S. is expected to lose more soybean market share to South America, perhaps permanently.

"This theory of short-term pain for gain down the road - that's just not working, and it won't work," said Ed Hodgson, who farms 1,500 acres of land in Rice County, Kansas. He's been a farmer since graduating from college in 1967.

When U.S. President Donald Trump announced plans in April to implement tariffs on most countries - particularly key trading partners such as China, Mexico and Canada - Hodgson saw it as a "disaster" for farmers. He was not confident in the promise that tariffs would bring better prices.

In the last five years, China has accounted for around 52% of soybean exports, he noted. This year, it has "not bought a bushel due to tariffs and the trade war," said Hodgson, who usually has around one-fourth of his acreage dedicated to growing soybeans. "Our export market to China has just decimated the price."

So far this year, China has not purchased any soybeans from the U.S. for the 2025-26 marketing year, according to data from the U.S. Agriculture Department.

Last year, as of Sept. 18, China had purchased 6.8 million metric tons of soybeans (1 metric ton is about 36.7 bushels), according to Ryan Urie, director and global head of crops and proteins at S&P Global Commodity Insights.

U.S. soybean export commitments in million metric tons.

Global protein demand has surged over the past three decades, and the U.S. is well positioned to help meet it - but retaliatory tariffs have "blunted" U.S. soybean growers' advantage, according to the American Soybean Association.

The combination of 20% retaliatory tariffs, as well as value-added taxes (VAT) and most favored nation (MFN) taxes, have pushed China's overall duty rate on U.S. soybeans to 34% in 2025, the industry group said. U.S. soybean prices for overseas buyers became "prohibitively more expensive" than South American soybean supplies ahead of the U.S. harvest this fall, it added.

China has increasingly gone to Brazil and Argentina because of the lower prices, said Hodgson. That's market share "we have lost and probably will never get back."

Shift away from the U.S.

China has actually been shifting its purchases of soybeans to Brazil over the past 15 years due to economics, said Arlan Suderman, chief commodities economist at StoneX.

When Brazil's real currency became weak, that stimulated expansion of the country's soybean production, which was then cheaper to buy than U.S. soybeans, he told MarketWatch.

"As Brazil expanded production, that meant China needed to buy that much less from the United States each year," Suderman said. At the same time, China has been building its reserve storage of soybeans to use during a time such as this, so it "now physically can divest itself entirely away from U.S. supplies if it chooses to do so," he added.

China has also been pumping billions of dollars into building Brazil's agricultural infrastructure for at least a decade, so that it could grow more soybeans and get those supplies to port in a more timely fashion, said Darin Newsom, senior market analyst at Barchart.

And if China predominately pulls soybeans from Brazil, that does not mean the rest of the world will come to the U.S. to buy the commodity, given the U.S.'s trade wars with other countries, he noted.

"The U.S. cannot make up for lost demand from China," said Newsom, who's also president of Darin Newsom Analysis, a commodity commentary and analysis provider.

Alternative demand sources

There is one aspect of the U.S. market that may help replace some, though not all, of the lost soybean demand.

The U.S. biofuel program for biomass-diesel production is on course to replace Chinese business for soybeans with domestic demand for the crop, said Suderman. Soybean oil is used in biodiesel production.

U.S. biomass-diesel production under the Renewable Fuel Standard was at 4,292.4 million gallons in 2023 - a significant rise from 1,471.7 million gallons 10 years earlier, according to a report from the American Soybean Association.

"Farmers will be dependent on domestic biofuel policy to assist in the large crop [of soybeans] that is anticipated," S&P Global Commodity Insights' Urie said in emailed commentary. "Biofuel policy will allow crush margins to increase and therefore incentivize domestic crushing."

"However, the U.S. will not be able to crush its way out of excessive supply, he added. "The crush capacity in the U.S. is not large enough to consume the soybeans that we would otherwise export."

And the soybean market "can't just flip the switch" to have biofuel demand make up for the lost Chinese demand, said Suderman. It will "take several years to build the infrastructure necessary to absorb the lost Chinese business."

Jack Scoville, vice president at the Price Futures Group, also pointed out that soybeans are already used to make biofuels and animal feed. Demand from those sectors won't be enough and can't be quickly expanded, he said.

China bought around half of U.S. soybean exports, and the "rest of world can't take that volume," he noted.

No solution in sight

For now, it's "too early to quantify the losses for farmers, but it will be substantial," said StoneX's Suderman.

"It will be a significant loss for the industry if China doesn't buy another bushel from us," he said. "It will create storage problems across the Midwest - especially in the northwestern Midwest, where harvested soybeans are typically immediately loaded on a train for the Pacific Northwest ports to be shipped to China."

'It will be a significant loss for the [soybean] industry if China doesn't buy another bushel from us.'Arlan Suderman, StoneX

The new farm-program payments under the Trump administration's One Big Beautiful Bill Act will help, but not until 2026 and 2027, and that's why Congress is considering a "stopgap payout" to farmers, Suderman said.

In a post on Truth Social on Wednesday, Trump said the "soybean farmers of our country are being hurt because China is, for 'negotiating' reasons only, not buying." He also said that he'll meet with China's president in four weeks, and that soybeans would be a major topic of discussion.

Trump is also considering providing $10 billion or more in aid to farmers, with funding potentially coming from tariff revenue, The Wall Street Journal reported Thursday, citing people familiar with the discussions.

U.S. soybeans harvested in good condition can be stored for a "relatively long time," but a farmer "can't pay his bills with soybeans sitting in storage," said Suderman.

If Trump isn't able to reach a trade agreement that forces China to buy U.S. soybeans in exchange for more favorable access to the vast U.S. consumer market, that means - at least in the near term - that soybean demand will fall far short of production, and prices will be vulnerable to falling much lower, he said.

Prices for soybeans were already at low levels in the U.S. Soybeans for November delivery (SX25) (S00) settled Wednesday at $10.13 a bushel. Based on the most active contract, prices are down by a modest 0.25% so far this year, but that's after losses of 22% in 2024 and nearly 15% in 2023, a Dow Jones Market Data analysis of FactSet data show.

Realistically, Hodgson said he's seeing prices of around $9 a bushel for his soybeans, whereas $14 to $15 would be a profitable level.

'There's a lot of reasons why food is not cheap - and it's not because farmers are making any money.'Ed Hodgson, Kansas farmer

Meanwhile, suppliers have increased input costs - which include fertilizer, chemicals, seeds and farming equipment - year after year, he said.

"I'm sure that everybody in the United States wants cheap food, but there's a lot of reasons why food is not cheap - and it's not because farmers are making any money," said Hodgson.

-Myra P. Saefong

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal. 




  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  10-02-25 1619ET


Copyright (c) 2025 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: Hasbro to Announce Third Quarter 2025 Earnings on October 23, 2025
data.description:
Hasbro to Announce Third Quarter 2025 Earnings on October 23, 2025

Hasbro, Inc. (NASDAQ: HAS) announced today that the company’s third quarter financial results will be released before the market open on Thursday, October 23, 2025. Hasbro will webcast its third quarter 2025 earnings conference call at 8:30 a.m. Eastern Time.

Certain financial and statistical information included in the webcast, such as information required by Regulation G, will be available at the time of the webcast on Hasbro’s Investor Relations website at https://investor.hasbro.com.

The webcast and the accompanying presentation slides will be available to investors and the media on Hasbro's Investor Relations home page at https://investor.hasbro.com. A replay of the call will be hosted at the same location approximately two hours following completion of the event and will be available for 12 months following the date of the call.

About Hasbro

Hasbro is a leading games, IP and toy company whose mission is to create joy and community through the magic of play. With over 100 years of expertise, Hasbro delivers groundbreaking play experiences and reaches over 500 million kids, families and fans around the world, through physical and digital games, video games, toys, licensed consumer products, location-based entertainment, film, TV and more.

Through its franchise-first approach, Hasbro unlocks value from both new and legacy IP, including MAGIC: THE GATHERING, DUNGEONS & DRAGONS, MONOPOLY, HASBRO GAMES, NERF, TRANSFORMERS, PLAY-DOH and PEPPA PIG, as well as premier partner brands. Powered by its portfolio of thousands of iconic marks and a diversified network of partners and subsidiary studios, Hasbro brings fans together wherever they are, from tabletop to screen.

For more than a decade, Hasbro has been consistently recognized for its corporate citizenship, including being named one of the 100 Best Corporate Citizens by 3BL Media, a 2025 JUST Capital Industry Leader, one of the 50 Most Community-Minded Companies in the U.S. by the Civic 50, and a Brand that Matters by Fast Company. For more information, visit https://corporate.hasbro.com or @Hasbro on LinkedIn.

© 2025 Hasbro, Inc. All Rights Reserved.

HAS-IR

Investor Contact: Fred Wightman | Hasbro, Inc. | hasbro_investor_relations@hasbro.com
Media: Abby Hodes | Hasbro, Inc. | hasbrobrandpr@hasbro.com

data.title: Keller Williams Honored Among America’s Most Admired Workplaces 2026 by Newsweek
data.description:
Keller Williams Honored Among America’s Most Admired Workplaces 2026 by Newsweek

Keller Williams Realty, LLC (KW), the world’s largest real estate franchise by agent count, was named to America’s Most Admired Workplaces 2026 ranking by Newsweek, celebrating top companies that set a high standard in culture, talent engagement, and inspiration.

This press release features multimedia. View the full release here: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20251002744003/en/

“Being named among the nation’s most admired workplaces affirms that when you put people first, business success follows," said Chris Czarnecki, CEO and President, KW.

“Our mission has always been to empower entrepreneurs through unmatched training, technology, and culture,” said Chris Czarnecki, CEO and President, KW. “Being named among the nation’s most admired workplaces affirms that when you put people first, business success follows.”

This recognition underscores KW’s commitment to a people-first culture, powered by industry-leading training through KW University and KW MAPS Coaching, and built on the time-tested models and systems from the best-selling books The Millionaire Real Estate Agent and SHIFT, the foundation of countless thriving real estate careers.

Additionally, KW’s support programs and initiatives available for associates, such as KW Cares, KW Communities, and RED Day, foster a sense of belonging and well-being.

The America's Most Admired Workplaces 2026 list highlights top companies that excel at attracting and retaining talent, based on a comprehensive analysis of employee feedback.

“When people consider their dream job, they often put companies they admire at the top of the list,” said Jennifer H. Cunningham, Editor-in-Chief, Newsweek. “These companies are a leading example of best workplace practices, creating environments that are admired by employees.”

The complete details on America's Most Admired Workplaces 2026 methodology can be found here.

About Keller Williams

Austin, Texas-based Keller Williams Realty, LLC is the world’s largest real estate franchise by agent count. It has more than 1,000 market center offices and 161,000 affiliated agents. The franchise is No. 1 in units and sales volume in the U.S.

Since 1983, the company has cultivated an agent-centric, technology-driven, and education-based culture that rewards affiliated agents. For more information, visit kwri.kw.com.

Media Contact: Darryl G. Frost
Director of Public Relations and Media Relations
darryl.frost@kw.com / 254-466-3627

data.title: Care For the Homeless (CFH) Celebrates 40 Years of Impact with Commemorative Breakfast Event in New York City
data.description:
Care For the Homeless (CFH) Celebrates 40 Years of Impact with Commemorative Breakfast Event in New York City

Care For the Homeless (CFH) Celebrates 40 Years of Impact with Commemorative Breakfast Event in New York City

PR Newswire

NEW YORK, Oct. 2, 2025

New York Times bestselling Author Adriana Trigiani to host celebration that includes a panel discussion moderated by Michael Gargiulo, Anchor, NBC 4 New York

NEW YORK, Oct. 2, 2025 /PRNewswire/ -- Care For the Homeless (CFH), an industry-leading non-profit dedicated to providing health care, residential, and social services to the homeless population in New York City, will celebrate its 40th anniversary with a commemorative breakfast event on Thursday, October 23, at 9:00 a.m. at Bohemian National Hall, 321 East 73rd Street. The event will honor four decades of care, innovation, and advocacy, for New York City individuals and families experiencing homelessness – while shining a spotlight on the leaders, partners, and community members driving meaningful change. Tickets and sponsorship information can be found HERE.

The program will feature New York Times bestselling author Adriana Trigiani as emcee, bringing her signature warmth and storytelling to the event. A centerpiece of the morning will be a panel discussion, "The Past, Present, and Future: How CFH is Leading the Fight Against Homelessness in New York City," moderated by Michael Gargiulo, Anchor, NBC 4 New York. The conversation will include insights from George Nashak, CFH President & CEO; Bobby Watts, former CFH CEO and now CEO of the National Health Care for the Homeless Council); and Jonathan Santos-Ramos, CFH Chief Program Officer, offering a powerful reflection on CFH's legacy and vision for the future.

CFH will present three awards recognizing extraordinary contributions to its mission:

  • Susan Neibacher Lifetime Impact Award – Bobby Watts, CEO of the National Health Care for the Homeless Council and former CFH CEO, for his lifelong leadership advancing health care access and ending homelessness.
  • Champion of Change Award – Katie Devine, Founder & Managing Principal of Prospect Housing Partners and Principal at Rockabill Consulting, for her work alongside CFH's Residential Services team to expand affordable and supportive housing across NYC.
  • Distinguished Service Award – Presented to the CFH Consumer Advisory Board, a 13-member group of individuals with lived experience of homelessness who advise CFH on programs and services, ensuring care remains rooted in the realities of lived experience.

Care For the Homeless was founded in 1985 as one of 19 pilot programs funded by the Robert Wood Johnson Foundation and The Pew Charitable Trusts. For forty years, CFH has been at the forefront of the fight against homelessness, providing accessible, high-quality health care, residential, and social services. With 17 health centers embedded directly within community spaces (soup kitchens, shelters and drop-in centers) across all five boroughs—five shelters of which are operated by CFH directly—CFH removes barriers to care and provides critical support to those who need it most.

In 2024, CFH provided 41,000+ health care visits to more than 12,000 patients, including 1,239 children

  • Delivered nearly 9,200 mental health visits
  • Operated shelters offering 750 beds across NYC
  • Helped 198 individuals transition into permanent housing
  • For additional impact information: 2024 Impact Report

"Our 40th anniversary is a powerful affirmation of our founding belief that by providing health care, shelter, and social services – three critical components – we are not just treating symptoms; we are building a foundation for a new life," said George Nashak, President & Chief Executive Officer, Care For the Homeless.  "We are immensely grateful to our dedicated staff, our partners, and the entire community for making this vital work possible over the past four decades."

"I spent close to 6 years in the shelter system, and towards the end it got very dark. I reached a low point...At the shelter, fortunately, they had an on-site Care For the Homeless health center," shared M.A. Dennis, formerly homeless New Yorker and CFH patient. "And that is what got me on the road to recovery as far as mental health. Because that was something I wasn't willing to address up until that point. Care For the Homeless helped me individually. They built me from the inside out. They got me to a point where I believed in myself, and from there, I was able to do what I needed to do."

The anniversary event will unite 200 plus community leaders, longtime supporters, and those CFH serves, to honor four decades of progress – and to look ahead to a future where homelessness in New York City is rare and brief.

CFH invites the public to join in celebrating this milestone by purchasing tickets, becoming a sponsor, or making a donation at www.careforthehomeless.org/legacy.

About Care For the Homeless (CFH)
Care For the Homeless is New York City's original Health Care for the Homeless program, founded in 1985 as one of 19 pilot programs funded by the Robert Wood Johnson Foundation and The Pew Charitable Trusts. Care For the Homeless believes homelessness is not a defining characteristic, but a temporary condition. By integrating high quality health care, shelter, and social services, we deliver a proven model – one rooted in dignity, compassion, and respect. Our work goes beyond providing services. We believe that every New Yorker has a right to a place they call home, and we are a committed advocate for policies to ameliorate, prevent and end homelessness. Learn more at www.careforthehomeless.org.

Media Contacts:
Ashley Friedman
ashley.friedman@zenogroup.com
570-239-9147

Julio Vincent Gambuto
jgambuto@cfhnyc.org
212-366-4459, ext 217 or 917-841-7219

View original content to download multimedia:https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/care-for-the-homeless-cfh-celebrates-40-years-of-impact-with-commemorative-breakfast-event-in-new-york-city-302574192.html

SOURCE Care For the Homeless

data.title: VLS Environmental Solutions Launches Breakthrough Mercury Disposal Technology
data.description:
VLS Environmental Solutions Launches Breakthrough Mercury Disposal Technology

VLS Environmental Solutions Launches Breakthrough Mercury Disposal Technology

PR Newswire

DEER PARK, Texas, Oct. 2, 2025

DEER PARK, Texas, Oct. 2, 2025 /PRNewswire/ -- VLS Environmental Solutions, a leader in sustainable environmental management, has unveiled a revolutionary, patent-pending technology for the permanent disposal of elemental mercury. This technology is the first and only RCRA-compliant disposal option for elemental mercury in the United States. This innovative solution represents a significant milestone in hazardous waste management and a pivotal shift in how mercury waste should be managed.

Treatment and disposal of elemental mercury will occur at VLS' Texas Molecular RCRA permitted Treatment, Storage, and Disposal Facility (TSDF) in Deer Park, Texas. The state-of-the-art facility has the capacity and existing infrastructure to safely treat and dispose of elemental mercury in accordance with regulations and existing permits. VLS Texas Molecular is fully permitted by both the Texas Commission on Environmental Quality (TCEQ) and the U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to accept and dispose of mercury at this facility. This process ensures safe and permanent disposal, providing a definitive, compliant solution for a previously unresolved environmental challenge.

"This is a watershed moment for mercury waste management," said John Magee, CEO of VLS Environmental Solutions. "Our process provides the first true domestic solution for mercury disposal since the Mercury Export Ban Act (MEBA) took effect in 2013. Government agencies and industry stakeholders now have a viable, compliant option for final disposal."

Addressing a Long-Standing Industry Challenge

Signed into law in 2008, MEBA prohibited the export of elemental mercury beginning in 2013, leaving industry and government entities without a regulatory-compliant disposal option. VLS Texas Molecular's technical team has now resolved this predicament, eliminating the need for indefinite storage and reducing risks to public health and the environment.

"Methods like indefinite long-term storage, or stabilization and landfill, just delay the problem and can pose potential serious risks," said Chris Lobue, Executive Vice President of Hazardous Waste. "There is a reason stabilization and landfill of mercury above the water table isn't allowed under current EPA regulations. The elegance of the VLS solution is that it fits within existing regulations and uses a proven technology that was originally designed to protect drinking water sources."

Meeting the Needs of Key Stakeholders

Our solution directly supports the needs of the mining, energy, chemical, and other manufacturing sectors. It can also support the needs of federal agencies that are currently managing legacy mercury reserves. These agencies and industries are no longer burdened with large unnecessary costs associated with storing their elemental mercury stockpiles in perpetuity.

"We're proud to offer a solution that balances sustainability with compliance," said Jimmy Bracher, Vice President of Hazardous Waste Sales. "This technology finally addresses a critical gap in disposal capabilities."

"This is more than a technological advancement—it's a paradigm shift in mercury waste management," said Robert Wheatley, Chief Strategy Officer. "Our solution enables industries to resolve mercury challenges domestically, creating both environmental and economic value."

Leading the Industry Forward

Operating since 1981, the VLS Texas Molecular Deer Park facility is the only site in the nation equipped to deliver this level of mercury waste management. The company's commitment to environmental leadership is reflected in its record for regulatory compliance and its approach to safety.

"The impact of this technology goes beyond regulatory compliance," said Keith Cordesman, President of VLS. "It's about protecting ecosystems, advancing sustainable development, and guiding industries toward a future built on responsible innovation."

For more information on our elemental mercury solution, call our experts at (877) 861-8588 or email marketing@vlses.com.

About VLS Environmental Solutions

VLS Environmental Solutions is a leading provider of environmental and sustainability services across North America. The company operates four divisions:

  • Non-Hazardous Waste Services: Offers innovative solutions for landfill diversion, recycling, wastewater treatment, and waste sequestration, ensuring efficient and sustainable waste processing.

  • Hazardous Waste Services: Specializes in the management, treatment, and disposal of hazardous materials using advanced technologies, like waste sequestration, and strict safety protocols to protect the environment and ensure compliance.

  • Rail Services: Provides comprehensive railcar cleaning, light and heavy repair, and field services for railcars transporting chemicals, hardened materials, and pressurized gases, with a focus on safety and efficiency.

  • Marine Services: Delivers advanced barge cleaning, repair, and gas-free services for chemical solvents and petroleum products, emphasizing environmental responsibility and operational excellence.

With over 1,500 employees across more than 50 locations, VLS is committed to delivering innovative, compliant, and sustainable solutions.

For more information about VLS, visit https://www.vlses.com/.

SOURCE VLS Environmental Solutions

data.title: Garantir Debuts Market-First Unlimited CLM + PKI to Slash Costs and Align Price-to-Value
data.description:
Unlimited use certificate lifecycle management and private PKI disrupt an overpriced, restrictive market by offering one simple subscription fee

SAN DIEGO, CA / ACCESS Newswire / October 2, 2025 / Garantir, a leader in enterprise cryptographic security, today announced the launch of its Unlimited Certificate Lifecycle Management (CLM) and Private Public Key Infrastructure (PKI) offering, designed to eliminate costly certificate outages, simplify compliance, and reduce enterprise PKI costs by up to six figures annually.

Garantir Logo

For decades, cryptography has been the backbone of digital trust. Yet managing the certificates that secure websites, applications, devices, and identities has only grown more complex. Certificates and keys are getting larger, renewals are becoming more frequent, and with new compliance changes set to take effect in March 2026, organizations will soon face a heavier operational burden than ever before. Despite these pressures, incumbent vendors continue to rely on outdated, per-certificate pricing models with confusing tiers and hidden add-ons that make enterprises feel held hostage by unpredictable, skyrocketing bills.

Breaking the Enterprise Pricing Barrier

Garantir's new model replaces per-certificate pricing with a simple, predictable subscription:

Unlimited CLM: $99K/year

Private PKI: $25K/year

Unlimited includes no cap on certificates or certificate types under management, full discovery, automated renewals, support for public third-party trusted and private CAs, policy enforcement, connectors, users, and teams - with no hidden fees, no tiers, and no per-certificate counting.

By removing artificial price barriers, certificates can be monitored and renewed on time, eliminating the risk of outages, audit failures, and service disruptions.

With new short-lived certificate requirements on the horizon, enterprises face an unprecedented operational burden to keep pace with accelerated renewals. Affordable, enterprise-scale CLM automation is no longer optional, it's essential to maintain trust and uptime.

"Short-lived certificate rules will drastically increase the renewal volume and complexity organizations must manage," said Trell Rohovit, Garantir CEO. "Traditional CLM hasn't evolved in line with technology. Automation and integration should have driven prices down years ago, but enterprises are still being charged like it's 2015. If companies can't automate at scale, they're exposed to outages, compliance failures, and unnecessary risk. We are aligning price-to-value to ensure automation is accessible and affordable so every enterprise can meet this challenge head-on."

Driving Security and Savings

Over the past decade, some of the world's largest and most trusted digital platforms have been unexpectedly taken offline due to expired or misconfigured digital certificates. Analysts have repeatedly noted that these incidents are not isolated mistakes, but systemic risks that highlight how fragile critical systems become when certificate lifecycle management is left to manual tracking or fragmented processes.

By shifting to Garantir's simple-fee model, enterprises can redirect up to six-figure savings to other important security initiatives such as post-quantum cryptography readiness, ransomware defense, code signing, privileged access management, secrets management, etc., accelerating progress toward broader security goals.

True Unlimited, Seamless Integration, Rapid Deployment

Unlimited with Garantir truly means unlimited. Certificates across the enterprise can be discovered, enrolled, renewed, and governed without counting, tiers, or hidden limits. This includes full support for standard enrollment protocols such as SCEP, EST, and ACME, native orchestration with enterprise applications and platforms, and integrations with leading trusted public CAs including, but not limited to, DigiCert, Sectigo, and GlobalSign, as well as private PKIs like Microsoft, AWS Private CA, Garantir CA, and EJBCA.

In addition, Garantir enables CLM for TLS with properly protected keys in HSMs as well as just-in-time provisioning.

With enterprise-scale capacity built in, organizations can consolidate certificate lifecycle management across diverse environments, ensure certificates are continuously monitored and renewed, and eliminate the risks of outages or audit failures.

Customers typically go live in days to weeks - not months - without re-engineering or costly consulting engagements.

By combining discovery, broad connector coverage, seamless app integration, HSM-protected keys, and just-in-time provisioning into a single predictable subscription fee, Garantir redefines what CLM + PKI should deliver for modern enterprises.

About Garantir
Garantir is a cybersecurity company that provides advanced cryptographic solutions to the enterprise. The Garantir team has worked on the security needs of businesses of all sizes, from startups to Fortune 500 companies. At the core of Garantir's philosophy is the belief that securing business infrastructure and data should not hinder performance or interrupt day-to-day operations. With GaraTrust, Garantir's flagship cryptographic services platform, private keys remain secured at all times, while a client-side hashing architecture ensures high performance for all cryptographic operations, including code signing, SSH, S/MIME, document signing, application-level data encryption, TLS, secure backup, and more. Explore https://www.garantir.io to learn more.

Contact Information

Shane Paris
Business Development Associate
shane.paris@garantir.io
9093448519

SOURCE: Garantir Cybersecurity
